primary_detection.name: Random/Meaningless Variable Names
primary_detection.description: Uses random alphanumeric strings as variable names to obfuscate code intent
evidence: a7x9k2 = 'curl' - Random alphanumeric name for command string
m3p8q = 'https://...' - Meaningless name for exfiltration URL
b9f2h, c4k7n, d6l8s - Random names for sensitive file paths
p2m9y, q8w1e, r7u4i - Random parameter and local variable names

underlying_threats.name: Credential Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: Reads /etc/passwd, SSH keys, and auth logs then sends to external server

underlying_threats.name: External Data Transmission
underlying_threats.description: Uses curl to POST collected data to external endpoint

underlying_threats.name: Auto-Execution on Import
underlying_threats.description: Malicious code runs automatically when module is imported

obfuscation_techniques.purpose: Prevent code review from identifying malicious intent
Avoid string-based detection of full command

verdict_reasoning: Random/meaningless variable names combined with clear data exfiltration behavior targeting sensitive system files. The obfuscation pattern is designed to hide credential theft and external transmission.
recommended_actions: Block all network requests to the external endpoint
Report pattern to security team for signature updates
